rationale: Qwen3.5-397B-A17B is a hybrid MoE model: 60 layers, of which 15 are full_attention (every 4th, full_attention_interval=4 -> idx 3/7/.../59) and 45 are linear_attention (GatedDeltaNet). RedKnot full-attention head-class sparsity is applied ONLY to the full layers. Shallow full layers carry critical early features, so we keep the FIRST HALF+1 full layers DENSE (exact) and sparsify only the DEEP half. Sweet spot from the 397B sweep: keeping the first 9 of 15 full layers dense + head-class (frac_global=0.4, window=2048) on the deep 6 is lossless (dF1=0) and maximizes compute saving. Note frac_global=0.4 pairs with window=2048 (window=4096 dropped F1 at this frac_global; frac_global=0.8 instead needs window=4096).

summary.note: Sweet spot selected on the 397B FP8 triviaqa sweep (lossless dF1=0, total compute save ~46%).

sweet_spot.selection: frac_global=0.4, window=2048, deep_moe_start=24, mass_thresh=0.7. Selected on the 397B reduced sweep (lossless dF1=0, max compute save). Verified on the FP8 model at 32K context (N=5, triviaqa): RedKnot outputs matched the STANDARD baseline (lossless) with 51.8% total compute saving and 2.07x theoretical TTFT speedup. mass_thresh=0.7 consistently saved more MoE compute without accuracy loss; deep_moe_start=24 slightly beat 40.
